ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar May-11-2016 Continued To May-25-16 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 514

Notice Of Motion And Motion In Equity To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Obtained Through Extrinsic Fraud, Mistake, Or Duress
Off Calendar Mar-24-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JULIAN T. LASTOWSKI, ESQ. of the Law Division of Fidelity National Title Group, Inc., Tel No. 925-817-3718 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JASON WOLFORD, ESQ. of Wolford Law Firm, Tel No. 415-265-2897 appearing for Defendany Nancy Mattingly.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT NANCY MATTINGLY'S Motion In Equity To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Obtained Through Extrinsic Fraud, Mistake, Or Duress is denied. In her supplemental declaration Ms. Mattingly states that she learned about the judgment against her in June 2015, yet this motion was not filed until February 18, 2016. One of the required elements for setting aside a default judgment on equitable grounds is that, once the default judgment is discovered, the moving party must establish that she acted diligently in seeking to set it aside. (Cruz v. Fagor America, Inc. (2007) 146 Cal. App. 4th 488, 503). Ms. Mattingly has not satisfied this required element. By analogy to Code of Civil Procedure 473(b), six months is the outer limit of reasonableness to seek to set aside a default judgment. Ms. Mattingly's declaration shows that there was nothing that prevented her from filing her motion earlier. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion In Equity To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Obtained Through Extrinsic Fraud, Mistake, Or Duress
Off Calendar Mar-11-2016 Continued To Mar-24-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JULIAN T. LASTOWSKI, ESQ. of Fidelity National Law Group, Tel No. 925-817-3718 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JASON WOLFORD, ESQ. of Wolford Law Firm, Tel No. 415-265-2897 appearing for Defendant Nancy Mattingly.

Matter argued by counsel. The Court vacates its tentative ruling and orders as follows: Due to defendant having submitted a supplemental declaration only today, the Court continues DEFENDANT NANCY MATTINGLY'S Motion In Equity To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Obtained Through Extrinsic Fraud, Mistake, Or Duress to March 24, 2016 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion. Plaintiff may file a supplemental opposition no later than 3/21/16 with a courtesy copy to be submitted to Dept. 302. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185 (302/HK)
